content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 19, 1990

Daniel H. Lowenstein  
University of California, Los Angeles  
School of Law  
405 Hilgard Avenue  
Los Angeles, CA 90024-1476

Re: Your Request For Informal Assistance  
Our File No I-90-628

Dear Professor Lowenstein:

You have requested assistance under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Because you have not provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you are seeking assistance, we consider your request to be one for informal assistance.2/

You have asked us to reconsider the advice previously stated in the Berman Advice Letter, No. A-88-283. That letter provides that a candidate or committee which purchases a position on a slate card produced by a slate mailer organization must itemize the expenditures made by the slate mailer organization in connection with the slate card.

Enclosed please find a copy of the Padberg Advice Letter, No. I-90-521. In this letter the advice previously issued in the Berman Advice Letter is modified to provide as follows:

Because slate mailer organizations have obligations under the Act to register and file campaign disclosure statements itemizing,

__________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

among other things, expenditures made for the production of slate mailers, a candidate or committee which purchases a position on a slate card from a slate mailer organization is not required to itemize expenditures made by the slate mailer organization. It is sufficient for the candidate or committee, when reporting the payment to the slate mail organization on Schedule E of its campaign disclosure statement, to indicate in the "Description" section of Schedule E that the payee is a slate mailer organization which files disclosure statements under the Political Reform Act.

Please note that this advice applies only to expenditures in connection with slate mailers sent by "slate mailer organizations," as defined in the Act, and does not apply to expenditures in connection with slate mailers sent by a candidate or committee, or any other entity.

If you have any questions about this letter, please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

Enclosure
